operational_knowledge_units.title: Fault display mapping: System vs AGV faults
operational_knowledge_units.summary: Faults are split: general system faults show on the left, AGV-related faults on the right. Example: if an AGV is commanded to move and no in‑progress/movement occurs, it appears on the right; communication-loss type items appear on the left.
observed_evidence: So if we tell an AGB to move and we haven't gotten in progress... it's a system fault...
On the left, you'll see system faults and on the right there's AGV system faults.
If we told an AGV to move but it hasn't moved, it'll be on the right.
relationships: Loss of communication -> listed under system faults (left panel).
AGV command not starting -> listed under AGV system faults (right panel).
Screen organizes faults by scope (system-wide vs AGV-specific).
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Fault screen: left = system faults (e.g., comms loss); right = AGV system faults. If an AGV is told to move but no in-progress/movement, it shows on the right.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Remote monitoring constraints and workarounds (RMS/Ignition)
operational_knowledge_units.summary: Thin-client environments limit remote visibility of RMS screens. Current workaround is to pull data via APIs or query the database to mirror on-screen info. Long term, replicating these views in Ignition is desired for easier remote access.
observed_evidence: Because they're on thin clients, we can't see our mess.
We're kind of dependent on either pulling an API...
…or using the database to see what's on their screen.
relationships: Thin clients -> restrict direct RMS screen sharing.
APIs/DB -> used to mirror RMS screen data for remote support.
Desired: replicate RMS views in Ignition for easier access.
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Remote RMS visibility is limited on thin clients; teams pull via API or database to see what’s on-screen. Goal: replicate views in Ignition for easier remote access.
